sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Exelon Corp. Stock (US30161N1019): Q1 2026 Earnings Release Scheduled for Today
feed.summary: Exelon Corp. is scheduled to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings today, April 30, 2026. Investors await updates on utility operations, regulatory matters, and previous guidance amid a strong U.S. economy, with the report expected to provide insights into the company's performance and execution of capital investment plans. Exelon, a major regulated U.S. utility holding company, is expected to reaffirm its 2026 earnings guidance.

feed.title: What's Going On With AEye Stock Today?
feed.summary: AEye, Inc. (NASDAQ: LIDR) announced a strategic partnership with SynTech to expand its defense applications, aiming to integrate advanced perception solutions into defense systems. This collaboration follows the appointment of Paul Berton as VP of Operations and Quality, intended to scale global manufacturing and improve supply-chain execution. Analysts maintain a "Buy" rating for LIDR with an average price target of $3.50, as the company anticipates significant revenue growth by May 2026.

feed.title: Magnitude International Posts Interim Loss as Project Activity Slows
feed.summary: Magnitude International Ltd (MAGH) reported a significant decline in its unaudited interim financial performance for the six months ended October 31, 2025, swinging to a net loss of S$1.51 million from a net profit of S$196,748 in the prior year. This downturn is attributed to reduced project activity, higher operational costs, and margin pressures within Singapore's mechanical and electrical contracting sector. The company's revenue also decreased by approximately 4.5% year-on-year to S$7.0 million.

feed.title: New York, USA: Lindblad Expeditions Schedules 2026 Q1 Financial Results Call for May 5
feed.summary: Lindblad Expeditions Holdings, Inc. will release its first-quarter 2026 financial results on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, before the market opens, followed by a conference call at 9:00 AM Eastern Time. This event is crucial for investors as it provides insight into the company’s operational performance and strategic outlook within the growing expedition travel sector. The earnings release and a live audio webcast of the call will be available on the Investor Relations section of Lindblad's official website, with a replay and transcript provided within 48 hours.

feed.title: Southern Co.: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Southern Co. (SO) announced strong first-quarter earnings, reporting $1.36 billion in net income, or $1.21 per share. Adjusted for non-recurring costs, earnings were $1.32 per share, exceeding Wall Street's expectation of $1.21 per share. The power company also surpassed revenue forecasts, posting $8.4 billion against an expected $8.09 billion.

feed.title: Hubbell: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Hubbell Inc. reported first-quarter earnings of $181.8 million, or $3.41 per share, with adjusted earnings of $3.93 per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The electrical products manufacturer also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $1.52 billion for the period. Hubbell projects full-year earnings to be between $19.30 and $19.85 per share.

feed.title: Microchip Technology Incorporated $MCHP Shares Sold by Shaker Investments LLC OH
feed.summary: Shaker Investments LLC OH reduced its stake in Microchip Technology Incorporated (NASDAQ:MCHP) by 22.0% in the fourth quarter, selling 13,628 shares. Despite this, other major investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and PNC Financial Services Group Inc. increased their holdings. The article also notes recent analyst ratings ranging from "Hold" to "Strong Buy" and mentions CEO Steve Sanghi's sale of over 400,000 shares.

feed.title: GC Wealth Management RIA LLC Sells 4,768 Shares of The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc $PNC
feed.summary: GC Wealth Management RIA LLC reduced its stake in The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc (NYSE:PNC) by 46.7% in the fourth quarter, selling 4,768 shares and holding 5,434 shares worth $1.13 million. Despite this, other institutional investors increased their positions, with 83.53% of the stock owned by hedge funds and institutions. The PNC Financial Services Group reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $1.70.

feed.title: Ford Lifts Full-Year Profit Outlook After Strong Q1
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has raised its full-year profit forecast, now expecting $8.5 billion to $10.5 billion before interest and taxes, an increase of $500 million, due to strong demand for high-margin pickups and SUVs. The automaker significantly exceeded first-quarter analyst expectations with adjusted earnings of 66 cents per share. Despite the positive outlook, Ford anticipates a $2 billion hit from rising commodity prices and burned through $1.9 billion in adjusted free cash flow due to investments in energy storage and electric vehicle development.

feed.title: GC Wealth Management RIA LLC Sells 14,119 Shares of United Airlines Holdings Inc $UAL
feed.summary: GC Wealth Management RIA LLC has reduced its stake in United Airlines Holdings Inc (UAL) by 57.2% in the fourth quarter, selling 14,119 shares and now owning 10,560 shares valued at $1,181,000. Other institutional investors have increased their positions in UAL, while President Brett J. Hart also sold shares. Analysts have a "Buy" consensus rating on UAL with an average price target of $132.18.

feed.title: U.S. Consumer American Express® Gold Card Introduces New and Enhanced Benefits as Part of 60th Anniversary Celebration
feed.summary: American Express has unveiled new and enhanced benefits for its Gold Card as part of its 60th-anniversary celebration. These updates include 5X Membership Rewards points on prepaid hotels booked through Amex Travel, complimentary Hertz Five Star status, and an expanded $120 Dining Credit, all while maintaining the $325 annual fee. The changes aim to catering to Card Members' evolving preferences for dining and travel.

feed.title: U.S. laser maker expands Italy base to serve European defense programs
feed.summary: nLIGHT, Inc. (Nasdaq: LASR) is expanding its operations in Torino, Italy, to meet the increasing demand from European and allied customers for directed energy solutions. This expansion will allow the company to assemble and support advanced laser solutions in the region, reducing delivery times and enhancing technical collaboration for defense applications. The initiative underscores nLIGHT's commitment to supporting allied defense and security needs through localized manufacturing and robust supply chains.

feed.title: Builders FirstSource authorizes $500M share buyback
feed.summary: Builders FirstSource (BLDR) has authorized a new share repurchase program, allowing the company to buy back up to $500 million of its common stock. This authorization is effective immediately and has no expiration date, signaling management's confidence in the company's financial health and future prospects.

feed.title: Lufax's SEC annual filing covers 2025, audited statements available
feed.summary: Lufax (NYSE: LU) has filed its Annual Report on Form 20-F with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on April 30, 2026, for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The report, which includes audited consolidated financial statements, is accessible on the company's investor relations website. Shareholders and ADS holders can also request a free hard copy of the report.

feed.title: A $4.02B quarter prompts ITW to lift its 2026 profit target
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) reported strong Q1 2026 results with revenue of $4.02 billion and GAAP EPS of $2.66, representing increases of 4.6% and 12% respectively. This robust performance led the company to raise its full-year 2026 GAAP EPS guidance to $11.10–$11.50 and project revenue growth of 2–4%. ITW also plans to repurchase approximately $1.5 billion in shares for 2026, highlighting a positive outlook.

feed.title: DTE Energy Posts Lower Q1 Earnings, Reaffirms 2026 Outlook
feed.summary: DTE Energy reported lower Q1 2026 earnings, with diluted EPS falling to $1.19 from $2.14 year-over-year, and operating earnings also decreased. Despite this, the company invested over $1.2 billion in utilities and is on track for $6 billion in 2026 spending on grid reliability, clean energy, and gas safety. DTE also reaffirmed its 2026 operating EPS guidance of $7.59 to $7.73, supported by a significant deal to power Google's data center and $400 million in electric distribution upgrades.

feed.title: Steris plc Stock (IE00BFY8C754): Next Earnings on February 4, 2026
feed.summary: Steris plc is set to release its next quarterly earnings on February 4, 2026, after market close. The company, which provides infection prevention and surgical products, reported $5.70 billion in revenue and $688.44 million in profits over the last 12 months. This event is significant for investors following the medical device and healthcare sector, especially given Steris plc's NYSE listing for U.S. investors and its focus on recurring revenue streams from services and consumables.

feed.title: PSE&G's Energy Efficiency Programs Show Significant Impact in New Jersey
feed.summary: PSE&G's energy efficiency programs have significantly impacted New Jersey, with 500,000 customers saving nearly $960 million annually and 22,000 businesses completing 35,000 energy-saving projects. These initiatives lead to substantial environmental benefits, avoiding over 2.4 million metric tons of carbon emissions annually. PSE&G plans to continue these collaborative efforts to ensure long-term value and advance sustainable development in the state.

feed.title: Elevance Health’s Affiliated Health Plans Deliver More Predictable, Lower Healthcare Costs for Small Businesses
feed.summary: Elevance Health's affiliated health plans are providing solutions like Balanced Funding and Multiple Employer Welfare Arrangements (MEWAs) to help small businesses manage rising healthcare costs. These programs offer greater cost stability, risk protection, and tools for preventive care, aiming to make healthcare more accessible and predictable for employers and their employees. Successful implementations in Kentucky and Ohio demonstrate significant cost savings and long-term stability for participating businesses.

feed.title: Bioprocessing Applications Laboratory Opened in Korea by Ecolab Life Sciences
feed.summary: Ecolab Life Sciences has launched a new bioprocessing applications lab (BPAL) in Dongtan, Korea, expanding its bioprocessing business. This facility aims to provide biopharmaceutical manufacturers across Asia with local access to downstream process development support, optimize chromatography steps, and accelerate regulatory pathways. It is Ecolab's first bioprocessing facility in Asia, complementing its existing network in the U.S. and U.K., and seeks to help manufacturers scale with speed while meeting regulatory and performance expectations.

feed.title: Columbus pickup-auction marketplace hits $4M run rate after launch
feed.summary: Retail Rush, a buy-online, pick-up-in-store (BOPIS) consumer auction marketplace, announced its Columbus, Ohio operation has reached a $4 million annual revenue run rate since its launch on September 1st. This milestone is attributed to repeat customers, daily online auctions of overstock and returned goods, and a local pickup model that eliminates shipping costs. The company plans to scale by increasing inventory, enhancing customer experience, and expanding into additional markets.

feed.title: First commercial rat IVF service targets breeding bottlenecks
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories has launched the first commercial in vitro fertility (IVF) service bundle for rat models, aiming to accelerate drug development across various therapeutic areas. This service leverages embryo transfer, cryopreservation, and colony rederivation to overcome breeding bottlenecks, reduce downtime, and safeguard critical genotypes. By expanding options for researchers, Charles River seeks to advance drug development and bring treatments to patients faster while upholding high industry standards.

feed.title: Snap-on Inc. Stock (US8330341012): Shares Gap Up on NYSE After Earnings Report
authors: AD HOC NEWS Editorial Team – Equity Coverage
feed.summary: Snap-on Inc. (US8330341012) saw its shares gap up on the NYSE following its quarterly earnings report, opening at $400.88 after closing at $382.38 the prior day. The company reported an EPS of $4.69, which missed analyst estimates by $0.08. Snap-on's core business model focuses on manufacturing and distributing tools and diagnostics to professional technicians, utilizing a direct sales model and emphasizing premium pricing for its durable products.

feed.title: Li Bang (NASDAQ: LBGJ) wins approval for share overhaul and 1-for-200 consolidation
feed.summary: Li Bang International Corporation Inc. (NASDAQ: LBGJ) shareholders have approved a comprehensive overhaul of the company's share capital and structure. This includes a significant increase in authorized Class B ordinary shares, a share capital reduction and reorganization, and authorization for a 1-for-200 reverse stock split at the board's discretion within two years. These measures grant the board broad flexibility to manage the company's capital structure and potentially alter share counts, per-share prices, and voting dynamics.

feed.title: MSCI executives take investor conference stage twice in May
feed.summary: MSCI Inc. (NYSE: MSCI) announced that two of its executives, Alvise Munari and Andy Wiechmann, will participate in investor conferences in May 2026. Munari will speak at the Barclays Annual Americas Select Conference on May 5, and Wiechmann will present at the Bernstein Institutional Services LLC Annual Strategic Decisions Conference on May 28. Live webcasts and replays of these events will be available on MSCI's Investor Relations homepage.

feed.title: Army taps Leidos to build AI systems for faster battlefield decisions
feed.summary: Leidos has secured an $869 million, five-year contract from the U.S. Army to develop and integrate AI-driven systems. These systems are designed to accelerate military decision-making across various domains, including electromagnetic, land, sea, air, space, and cyber. The initiative aligns with Leidos' NorthStar 2030 strategy, focusing on digital modernization and critical mission software development.

feed.title: Leidos receives $869 million Army contract to accelerate military decision making
feed.summary: Leidos has been awarded an $869 million, five-year MACRO II contract by the U.S. Army to develop and integrate secure systems using AI and advanced networking. The goal is to enhance warfighters' decision-making speed and accuracy across multiple domains. This contract aligns with Leidos' NorthStar 2030 strategy, focusing on digital modernization and critical mission software development.

feed.title: KKR Makes Strategic Investment to Accelerate Growth of MLS NEXT Pro
feed.summary: Major League Soccer (MLS) and KKR have announced a strategic investment to boost the growth of MLS NEXT Pro, forming Hometown Soccer Holdings (HSH) to serve as its commercial engine. HSH will focus on stadium development, creating new club brands, and enhancing fan experiences. This partnership aims to strengthen player development across the U.S. and Canada and expand professional soccer into new markets.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange raises quarterly dividend 8% to $0.52
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) has announced an 8% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.52 per share, payable on June 30, 2026. The company, which operates financial market technology and data services including the New York Stock Exchange, is considered undervalued by InvestingPro. This news follows recent positive analyst coverage and comes amid an ongoing CFTC investigation into oil futures trading on platforms operated by ICE and CME Group.

feed.title: FIS and Leading Financial Institutions to Build Their Own Digital Tokenized Money Network
feed.summary: FIS, in collaboration with six U.S. banks including Citizens, Fifth Third, and KeyBank, has launched Project Keystone. This initiative will create a bank-owned and bank-administered network for digital money, built on regulated deposits. The project aims to give participating banks direct control over the issuance, transfer, and settlement of digital money, ensuring it remains regulated and bank-issued, not a new asset class.

feed.title: ON Semiconductor Corp. Hits New 52-Week High of $101.25
feed.summary: ON Semiconductor Corp. has reached a new 52-week high of $101.25, demonstrating strong performance in the Other Electrical Equipment industry with a 34.5% one-year gain, outperforming the S&P 500. The company, with a market capitalization of USD 33,709 million, exhibits solid financial health, marked by a low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.06 and a return on equity of 10.06%. This milestone follows its previous 52-week low of USD 37.19, underscoring significant growth.

feed.title: PARKER-HANNIFIN ($PH) Releases Q3 2026 Earnings
feed.summary: PARKER-HANNIFIN ($PH) reported its Q3 2026 earnings, posting $8.17 per share, which exceeded estimates, but revenue of $5.486 billion missed analyst expectations. Insider trading activity for PH shows significant sales by executives, with no purchases in the last six months. The article also provides details on hedge fund and congressional trading activity, government contracts, and recent analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: A Look At Extra Space Storage (EXR) Valuation After Q1 Core FFO Beat And Guidance Update
feed.summary: Extra Space Storage (EXR) reported a Q1 core FFO beat and maintained 93% same-store occupancy, leading to recent share price momentum. Despite a relatively flat 1-year return, the stock appears undervalued compared to intrinsic value estimates and analyst targets. The company's valuation is boosted by growing ancillary income and an expanding third-party management platform, though risks like higher property taxes and market oversupply could impact margins.

feed.title: Walmart (NYSE: WMT) issues $4.2B in senior notes due 2029–2036
feed.summary: Walmart Inc. has announced the issuance of $4.23 billion in multi-tranche senior unsecured notes, comprising both floating-rate and fixed-rate notes with maturities ranging from 2029 to 2036. This offering diversifies Walmart's refinancing dates and interest-rate exposure, with net proceeds totaling approximately $4.23 billion before expenses. The notes rank equally with Walmart's other senior unsecured debt, and the issuance is being conducted under an existing automatic shelf registration statement.

feed.title: Builders FirstSource Posts Q1 Loss Amid Housing Slowdown
feed.summary: Builders FirstSource (BLDR) reported a net loss of $47.4 million in Q1 2026, a significant drop from a $96.3 million profit a year prior, as net sales declined 10.1% to $3.3 billion due to a weak housing market and commodity deflation. Despite increased leverage, the company maintained strong liquidity and expanded its share repurchase program with an additional $500 million authorization. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates BLDR as "Neutral," citing deteriorating financial performance and a bearish technical setup, but acknowledges potential support from cost controls and digital momentum.

feed.title: L3Harris lifts 2026 profit forecast on strong weapons demand
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies increased its 2026 profit forecast after exceeding first-quarter revenue estimates, driven by strong demand for weapons and military intelligence systems from the Pentagon. The defense supplier's missile solutions segment saw an 18% revenue rise, and the company confidentially filed for an IPO of this unit, supported by a $1 billion Department of Defense investment. Its space and mission systems business also experienced a 24% increase in first-quarter sales.

feed.title: JPMorgan cuts Fair Isaac stock price target on conservative guidance By Investing.com
feed.summary: JPMorgan lowered its price target on Fair Isaac (NYSE:FICO) stock to $1,225 from $1,325 but maintained a Neutral rating, citing the company's conservative full-year guidance for fiscal 2026. Despite a 37% drop in stock price over six months, InvestingPro analysis suggests the shares are undervalued. JPMorgan's estimates for FICO's revenue and EPS for fiscal 2026 are significantly higher than the company's guidance, reflecting management's historical pattern of setting beatable outlooks.

feed.title: Lexeo Therapeutics Adds Genomic Medicine Leader to Board
feed.summary: Lexeo Therapeutics, Inc. (LXEO) announced the appointment of Dr. Laura Sepp-Lorenzino to its board of directors and the Science and Technology Committee, effective April 28, 2026, with her term extending through the 2028 annual meeting. This move aims to leverage her extensive experience in genomic medicine from prominent biopharmaceutical companies and current board positions. Dr. Sepp-Lorenzino will receive standard compensation, including equity awards, to align her interests with long-term shareholder value and reinforce Lexeo's commitment to scientific leadership.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price trades higher as Q1 fees rise despite fall in AUM (TROW:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) shares are trading higher in pre-market due to an increase in Q1 investment advisory fees. This rise in fees occurred despite a sequential decrease in the company's Assets Under Management (AUM).

feed.title: Prudential updates total voting rights and share capital as of end of April
feed.summary: Prudential PLC announced its total issued share capital and voting rights as of April 30, 2026. The company has 2,521,242,327 ordinary shares, each carrying one vote, and holds no treasury shares. This figure is crucial for shareholders to determine their notification obligations under UK financial regulations.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price (TROW) Q1 2026: EPS $2.23 Beats $2.20 Est., Revenue $1.857B Misses $1.858B Est. -- Still 18.7% Undervalued? GF Score 84/100
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group Inc (TROW) announced its Q1 2026 earnings, reporting GAAP diluted EPS of $2.23, surpassing the $2.20 estimate, while adjusted diluted EPS reached $2.52. Despite beating EPS expectations, net revenues of $1.857 billion fell slightly short of the $1.858 billion estimate. The firm's ending AUM declined to $1.710 trillion due to market depreciation and client outflows, predominantly from equity strategies, though operating leverage and disciplined expense control supported overall earnings growth.

feed.title: Best Buy Stock Hits 52-Week Low at $59.76 Amid Retail Pressures
feed.summary: Best Buy Co., Inc. shares have fallen to a 52-week low of $59.76, signaling significant challenges in the consumer electronics retail sector. This decline is attributed to broader macroeconomic factors like inflation, cautious consumer behavior, and the Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady, impacting discretionary spending. The situation suggests potential promotional opportunities for consumers but poses risks for investors in volatile retail stocks.

feed.title: After a $1.12B quarter, Lincoln Electric returned $101M to shareholders
feed.summary: Lincoln Electric Holdings, Inc. reported strong first-quarter 2026 results with net sales increasing 11.7% to $1.121 billion and diluted EPS growing to $2.47. The company's performance was driven by organic sales growth and effective cost management. Lincoln Electric also returned $101 million to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, underscoring its commitment to capital allocation and driving shareholder value.

feed.title: Union Pacific argues for its $85B acquisition of Norfolk Southern in new railroad merger application
feed.summary: Union Pacific has filed a new application for its $85 billion acquisition of Norfolk Southern, hoping to convince regulators of the merger's benefits after an initial rejection. The Omaha-based railroad projects the merger will reduce delivery times, shift 2.1 million truckloads to trains, and create over 1,200 new jobs, with existing employees guaranteed jobs for life. However, competitors BNSF and CPKC, along with various trade groups and unions, have formed a coalition expressing concerns that the deal could lead to higher rates for shippers and harm the competitive balance, despite Union Pacific's claims that it will not dramatically alter market domination.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Remains a Buy on Visa (V)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has reaffirmed a Buy rating on Visa (V) with a $412 price target, citing strong quarterly revenues and net profits. Despite a negative corporate insider sentiment with increased selling activity, the overall analyst consensus suggests a Strong Buy for Visa with a significant upside potential from its current stock price. Citi also reiterated a Buy rating with a $400 price target, underscoring continued confidence in the company.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) Q1 2026 earnings hit $2.2B on strong cash flow
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips reported Q1 2026 net income of $2.2 billion, or $1.78 per share, with adjusted earnings of $2.3 billion, or $1.89 per share. The company generated $4.3 billion in cash from operations and returned $2.0 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Production averaged 2,309 MBOED and the company provided guidance for Q2 and full-year 2026 production and capital spending.

feed.title: Lincoln Electric: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Lincoln Electric Holdings Inc. reported first-quarter net income of $136.4 million, or $2.47 per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. Adjusted earnings were $2.50 per share. The company's revenue of $1.12 billion also exceeded forecasts by analysts.

feed.title: Karat Packaging (KRT) Expected to Announce Earnings on Thursday
feed.summary: Karat Packaging (NASDAQ:KRT) is scheduled to announce its Q1 2026 earnings on Thursday, May 7th, with analysts anticipating earnings of $0.32 per share and revenue of $112.9830 million. The company previously exceeded estimates in its last quarterly report, posting $0.34 EPS against a $0.28 consensus, and had revenue of $115.62 million. Karat Packaging also recently declared a quarterly dividend of $0.45 per share, representing an annualized dividend of $1.80 and a yield of 6.3%.

feed.title: Lifetime Brands (LCUT) to Release Quarterly Earnings on Thursday
feed.summary: Lifetime Brands (NASDAQ:LCUT) is scheduled to release its Q1 2026 earnings before the market opens on Thursday, May 7th. Analysts anticipate the company will report earnings of ($0.18) per share and revenue of $138.8580 million. The company's stock recently opened at $6.66 and has seen various institutional investors adjust their positions, while analysts generally have a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $5.00.

feed.title: Marathon Digital (MARA) Expected to Announce Quarterly Earnings on Thursday
feed.summary: Marathon Digital (MARA) is projected to release its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Thursday, May 7th, with analysts expecting a loss of ($1.4085) per share and revenue of $181.8580 million. The company reported a miss in its previous quarter, with EPS of ($4.52) against an estimated ($0.23) and revenue of $202.31 million against $250.74 million. Insider activity shows CEO Frederick G. Thiel and CFO Salman Hassan Khan recently sold shares, while institutional investors have adjusted their holdings.
